Spring Data Redis
  1. org.springframework.data.redis.connection
  2. BitFieldSubCommands

Uses of Class
org.springframework.data.redis.connection.BitFieldSubCommands

Packages that use BitFieldSubCommands
Package
Description
org.springframework.data.redis.connection
Connection package providing low-level abstractions for interacting with the various Redis 'drivers'/libraries.
org.springframework.data.redis.connection.lettuce
Connection package for Lettuce Redis client.
org.springframework.data.redis.core
Core package for integrating Redis with Spring concepts.

    Create new BitFieldSubCommands adding given BitFieldSubCommands.BitFieldGet to the sub commands.

    protected BitFieldSubCommands

    Create new BitFieldSubCommands adding given BitFieldSubCommands.BitFieldIncrBy to the sub commands.

    Create new BitFieldSubCommands adding given BitFieldSubCommands.BitFieldSet to the sub commands.

    Set the value.

    Method
    Description
    List<Long>
    DefaultStringRedisConnection.bitfield(String key, BitFieldSubCommands operation)
     
    List<Long>
    StringRedisConnection.bitfield(@NonNull String key, @NonNull BitFieldSubCommands command)
    Get / Manipulate specific integer fields of varying bit widths and arbitrary non (necessary) aligned offset stored at a given key.
    default List<Long>
    DefaultedRedisConnection.bitField(byte[] key, BitFieldSubCommands subCommands)
    Deprecated.
    in favor of RedisCommandsProvider.stringCommands()}.
    List<Long>
    DefaultStringRedisConnection.bitField(byte[] key, BitFieldSubCommands subCommands)
     
    default Mono<List<Long>>

    Get / Manipulate specific integer fields of varying bit widths and arbitrary non (necessary) aligned offset stored at a given key.
    List<Long>

    Get / Manipulate specific integer fields of varying bit widths and arbitrary non (necessary) aligned offset stored at a given key.
    ReactiveStringCommands.BitFieldCommand
    ReactiveStringCommands.BitFieldCommand.commands(BitFieldSubCommands commands)
    Applies the BitFieldSubCommands.
